modules.summary: Provides the authentication form allowing users to enter credentials or access the site as a guest and shows inline errors for invalid submissions.
modules.where_it_fits: The Login module is the entry point to the application and precedes access to the top navigation and course/dashboard areas; successful authentication redirects the user to the Dashboard where the top navigation bar and subsequent course navigation become available.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is not authenticated (logged out) and has access to the login page

modules.summary: The Dashboard provides a personalized landing page that displays a Timeline of upcoming teaching actions and a Calendar view of events across the user’s courses.
modules.where_it_fits: The Dashboard is reachable from the top navigation bar (Home, Dashboard, My Courses) and serves as the user's personalized landing area after login; it aggregates course activity and links into the dedicated calendar view and calendar import/export workflows. Within the course lifecycle it sits at the global level (outside individual Course pages) as an overview and navigation hub to course-specific activity and the full calendar.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated user session (logged in); user account exists and may be enrolled in one or more courses to populate Timeline and Calendar.

modules.summary: A mode for the Dashboard that enables adding, configuring, moving, and deleting page blocks with layout changes persisted per user and an option to reset the page to default.
modules.where_it_fits: This module is reached from the top navigation via the Dashboard link and activates while viewing the Dashboard page; it is part of the user-facing customization workflow that lets users personalize their Dashboard layout within the application's main navigation. It complements other course- and activity-level editing areas but specifically controls the Dashboard's block layout and content.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in user on the Dashboard page with permission to edit the Dashboard (Edit mode available)

modules.summary: Displays all courses a teacher has access to as visual cards with filtering, searching, sorting, layout options, and per-course actions (star or remove from view).
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the top navigation via My Courses (alongside Home and Dashboard), this page provides an index of the user's courses and serves as the entry point before navigating into a specific Course Page via the course name link.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated user (teacher) with access to one or more courses.

modules.summary: Displays a selected course’s full content organized into collapsible sections with activities and resources listed and a course-level tab bar.
modules.where_it_fits: Reached from the top navigation (e.g., My Courses or Dashboard) when a user opens a specific course, this is the primary course view located beneath the course title and horizontal tab bar (Course, Participants, Grades, Activities, Competencies) and works with the Course Index sidebar to navigate sections and activities. It is the main lifecycle screen for viewing course materials and activity links.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in and has opened a specific course (the course exists and the user has permission to view it).

modules.module_title: Course Edit Mode and Activity Chooser
modules.summary: Provides an authoring interface on the Course page that exposes inline editing controls for sections and activities and a modal Activity Chooser for adding new activities or resources.
modules.where_it_fits: This module is part of the Course Page experience: when a user viewing a course (via Home > My Courses or Dashboard > My Courses) enables Edit mode, the course page switches to this authoring UI and the Activity Chooser modal is launched from the "+ Add an activity or resource" control within course sections.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in, viewing a specific Course page, and has instructor/teacher permissions (ability to edit the course).

modules.summary: A multi-panel form for configuring all settings of a course assignment including availability, submission and feedback types, grading, access restrictions, completion, and notifications.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessed from the Activity Chooser while in a course (typically via Course Edit Mode) and used during course authoring; upon save it creates the assignment and returns to or opens the assignment page within the Course Page where it appears in the Course Index and activity breadcrumbs. It is part of the course content creation flow reachable from the course interface (Course tab, Course Index sidebar, and edit controls).
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is in a specific Course with editing enabled and has permission to add activities (e.g., logged in as a Teacher) and the Activity Chooser has been opened.

modules.summary: Provides a collapsible form for configuring course metadata, appearance, format, enrollment and completion settings, and media with inline validation and save/cancel actions.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessed from a course's horizontal tab bar (the Settings tab shown to teachers) when viewing a course; saving returns to the course page while canceling leaves settings unchanged. It sits alongside other course-level tools such as Participants, Grades, and Activities and is part of the course configuration lifecycle.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in, viewing an existing course, and has teacher/editing permissions (Settings tab visible).

modules.summary: Displays and manages the list of users enrolled in a specific course, providing filters, role editing, enrollment dialog, per-user actions, and bulk operations.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from within a course via the horizontal tab bar as the Participants tab (Course -> Participants) and sits alongside Course, Grades, and Activities in the course management workflow; it is used when managing enrollment and participant settings for a given course.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in and viewing a specific course page; the course exists and the user has permissions to view/manage participants (e.g., teacher or manager role) if enrollment or role changes are required.

modules.summary: Displays an assignment's metadata, attached files, read-only grading summary metrics, and navigation for grading, submissions, settings, and advanced grading from the teacher perspective.
modules.where_it_fits: Shown when viewing an assignment activity within a course (accessible via the course page and Course Index and represented in breadcrumbs like Course → Assignment); it sits in the activity-level workflow where teachers review assignment details and launch grading or submission management.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in as a Teacher and viewing a course that contains the assignment (the assignment activity already exists).

modules.summary: Displays a table of all student submission records for an assignment with search/filter controls, per-student actions to grade, and optional quick inline grading.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from within a course's activity view for an Assignment (breadcrumbs show the path from the course), and reachable via the Course tab and Course Index sidebar when an Assignment activity is selected. It is part of the grading workflow that teachers use after students submit work and complements the Gradebook and Assignment — Teacher View modules.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is viewing a course that contains the specific Assignment activity and is logged in with a role that has grading/teacher permissions; the assignment has at least one student submission (or is ready to receive submissions).

modules.summary: A spreadsheet-style report showing enrolled students as rows and activities as columns with filtering, per-cell and per-column editing, inline save, and class averages.
modules.where_it_fits: Reached from a course's horizontal tab bar under Grades (and also via the user menu's Grades link), the Grader report sits in the grading workflow as the teacher-facing interface for reviewing and editing grades after activities and submissions exist.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in as a teacher and viewing a course that has enrolled students and graded activities/submissions.

modules.summary: Displays the teacher's profile information including name and initials, contact and privacy details, links to related course profiles and content, reports, and login activity.
modules.where_it_fits: This page is reached from the top navigation's user menu (which contains links for Profile, Grades, Calendar, Private Files, Reports, and Preferences) and sits within the account-related area of the site alongside other personal pages; it complements course-level pages (e.g., Course Page and Participants) by providing the user's global information and links to their course profiles.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated session as a teacher (logged in)

modules.summary: Allows a user to edit their personal profile information, upload or change their user picture, and modify optional/custom fields via a multi-section form with validation.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the top navigation user menu under Profile, this module is part of the user account management flow and is reached from the global site header; after successful updates it returns the user to their Profile page. It sits alongside other account-related screens like Profile, Profile Edit, and Preferences in the user settings lifecycle.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in user who has opened their Profile page and has permission to edit their profile

modules.summary: Ends the current authenticated session and redirects the user to the login page.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the user menu in the top navigation bar (right-side user initials dropdown) and used as the final step in the session lifecycle; after activation the app returns to the Login page and protected areas (Dashboard, Course pages, etc.) require re-authentication. It is a global-level action available from any page via the top navigation.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is currently authenticated (logged in) with access to the top navigation user menu.
